probes.description: Send a tools/list request with an Origin header from a cross-origin host. A CORS wildcard (Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *) on the MCP endpoint allows any web page to make authenticated MCP requests using the victim's credentials, enabling cross-site request forgery at the MCP protocol layer.

assertions.description: CORS wildcard must not be set on the MCP endpoint; use an explicit allowlist of trusted origins

probes.description: CORS policy must also restrict the initialize handshake. A wildcard on /mcp/ applies to all methods including initialize.

assertions.description: CORS wildcard must not be present on the initialize response

remediation: Replace Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * on the MCP endpoint with an explicit allowlist of trusted origins (e.g. your front-end domain). Never combine wildcard CORS with credentialed requests. Consider adding a CSRF token requirement for state-mutating MCP calls if the server is browser-accessible.
